campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear from the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority about their investment performance for March 2026. The council also discussed adopting a new policy that prevents the city from allowing federal civil enforcement activities on city property, and addressed efforts to fill open city jobs and improve employee retention.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's March 2026 investment report
New policy restricting federal civil enforcement on city-owned property
Current city job openings and employee recruitment efforts
Employee retention strategies and compensation reviews

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a meeting to address dangerous or unhealthy conditions on certain properties in Saratoga that the county identified. They heard objections from residents, voted to approve cleanup orders on those properties, and also adopted new policies about technology and how residents can request special accommodations from the city.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanup orders
County-identified properties requiring abatement
New Technology Disruption Policy adoption
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and handle several maintenance and service matters. Key decisions included declaring overgrown brush and vegetation a public nuisance that needs to be cleared, approving a report on landscape and lighting maintenance, and signing long-term service agreements to keep city infrastructure maintained.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation (brush) declared a public nuisance - residents may need to clear overgrown brush on their properties
Landscape and lighting maintenance report approved
New 5-year service agreements authorized with contractors (specific vendor names incomplete in agenda)
Various contract amendments and extensions for city services

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare for their upcoming May 19th meeting by reviewing what items should be on the agenda and whether any need special time slots. They also discussed the meeting schedule for the next fiscal year and made appointments of council members to various city committees and boards.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 19, 2026 council meeting agenda
Set meeting times for upcoming fiscal year 2026-2027
Appointed council members to city committees and boards
Reviewed travel request from Councilmember Foley

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 12, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a private meeting to discuss two legal matters: a potential lawsuit related to a water main problem at Cuesta Park, and negotiations about buying or leasing two properties on Clyde Avenue. These closed-door discussions involved the city's legal team and real estate staff.
keyTopics: Cuesta Park water main incident - potential lawsuit
Property negotiations for 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue
Legal strategy and liability discussion
Real estate acquisition or lease terms

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters that aren't open to the public, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda provided limited details about what was actually discussed, focusing mainly on procedural items like the flag salute and meeting adjournment.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not publicly disclosed)
Small business agenda item
Special order of business
Regular council convening at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial review meeting about Cupertino's spending plan for the 2026-27 fiscal year. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city on legislation or decisions to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
City of Cupertino lobbying ordinance (Chapter 2.100)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on May 11, 2026 to discuss economic development and marketing efforts for the city. The council is making it easy for residents to participate either in person or remotely through Zoom or phone.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee (Ad Hoc) meetings
Remote participation options via Zoom and phone
In-person meeting attendance available
Public comment submission process (email by 11 AM day of meeting)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's finances and development for the next several years. Major decisions included approving a five-year plan for infrastructure projects, reviewing the upcoming year's budget, and adopting a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) for city infrastructure
Reviewed FY 2026-27 operating budget proposal
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council meeting procedures and handbook
Approved previous meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing about a plan to build a six-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be affordable housing for lower-income residents, and the developer is asking for special exemptions to make the project work on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 new affordable housing units on a small downtown lot
Six-story building replacing 52-space city parking lot
Developer requesting density bonus waivers under state law
Environmental review status being determined
